subject: Sociology
title: Roles and Status
description: A 60-second lesson on Roles and Status.

hook.text: You're a student, a sibling, an employee, and a friend — all before lunch. Each role carries different expectations.

buildup.text: A status is a social position. A role is the behavior expected of someone in that position.

discovery.text: Ascribed status is assigned at birth (race, sex). Achieved status is earned (doctor, criminal, graduate).

twist.text: Role conflict happens when your roles clash. Being a tough boss and a caring parent may demand opposite behaviors.

climax.text: We don't just play roles. Roles play us. They shape how we think, feel, and see ourselves over time.

punchline.text: You think you choose your roles. Often, they choose you.

quiz.question: What is the difference between ascribed and achieved status?
options: Ascribed is assigned at birth; achieved is earned
Ascribed is earned; achieved is inherited
